blockers: claude has required edits: Update stale header comments: inventory.mjs, util.mjs, git.mjs, and code.mjs all say 'plan v12 Task 4a' but this review is against v13 (v12 + AM-40). Either reference v13 or the frozen-scope identifier so the committed provenance comment matches the authorizing plan version.; AM-40 requires a unit that 'mechanically discovers every directory in the repository holding a TRACKED package.json' — test-inventory.mjs section 7 walks the filesystem (readdirSync) rather than consulting git tracking (e.g. git ls-files). An untracked/stray package.json directory would fail the suite even though it is not a tracked package, and conversely the check does not prove discovery over TRACKED files as the frozen text specifies. Rework the completeness unit to enumerate tracked package.json paths (or justify equivalence and pin it), and assert discovered == PACKAGE_ROOTS \u222a PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE as a set equality in BOTH directions plus the disjointness check (the current per-element loops do achieve both directions, but the tracked-vs-filesystem basis must match the ruling).; The counted-iterator semantics deviate from the frozen Task 4a text: the plan says the count increments 'only when the consumer completes consumption of that source (open + read + processing to edge-extraction eligibility without fatal error)' and the units must prove 'a fully consumed one is counted exactly once.' makeCountedSource counts on the consumer's RESUME, so a consumer that fully processes the last item and then stops without resuming records N-1 — the implementation itself documents this divergence as a 'RATIFIED Task-4a contract' and pins it in test-util section 8. That ratification is not in the frozen v13 text; a fully-consumed final source that is never followed by a resume is 'fully consumed' under the plan's definition but is not counted. Either (a) obtain an explicit amendment authorizing the resume-counting semantics, or (b) restructure the iterator so completion of the final item's processing is countable (e.g. count on the next() call that returns done, plus documented equivalence for for...of full-consumption), keeping the partial-consumption-not-counted property.; test-closure.mjs section 5's 'missing member fails' case only exercises omission of a member reached via a plain named static import within that block; the frozen text requires closure-equality failure fixtures where the omitted member is reached ONLY through re-export, literal dynamic import, or an accepted require-style form. Section 2 does cover omission per form via inventoryMatches(['clotho/entry.mjs'],...) across all eight forms, which arguably satisfies this — but the export-from/export-star/dynamic/require omission fixtures rely on the same generic harness; add an explicit assertion or comment binding section 2's per-form omission checks to the required failure-fixture list so the coverage mapping is auditable.; git.mjs's parseShaLines accepts CRLF terminators and treats them as valid; the frozen text says 'Every output line is validated as a full SHA' and 'malformed output fatal' without authorizing CRLF normalization. This is a defensible platform accommodation (Windows) and bare-CR is still fatal, but it is an interpretation beyond the letter of the frozen scope — record it as a reviewed, pinned decision (it is pinned in test-git section 6) in the PR description or an amendment note rather than silently in code comments.

claude has hard stops: Counted-iterator counting semantics (resume-counting; N-1 on last-item-then-break) self-describe as a 'RATIFIED Task-4a contract' but no such ratification exists in the frozen v13 Task 4a text or decision table supplied for this review; the frozen text's completion definition ('processing to edge-extraction eligibility without fatal error') does not make counting contingent on a subsequent resume. An unauthorized semantic redefinition of D26/D29 accounting is a scope/faithfulness defect that must be either amended by The Eye or corrected before this seat can sign.

codex has required edits: Replace or repair the shared lexer/classifier so it recognizes exactly the accepted D33 forms using declaration/call boundaries and sound token context, without keyword bleed or regex-vs-division omissions. Add closure tests for postfix-update division followed by literal `import()`, non-load `import`/`export` property-name lookalikes, and other corrected boundary cases.; Make `scanExports` recognize actual export declarations only. Make `scanImports` return the complete declaration span, including import attributes/assertions, and add a unit proving an imported local appearing only inside its own declaration remains unused.; Apply the canonical repository-relative POSIX validator to every successful resolver and closure output, including the entry path, and fail fatally on noncanonical names.; Handle only genuine absence errors as missing. All other `lstat`/directory-walk errors must fail closed; `physicalContainment` must return false or throw when any component cannot be inspected. Add units for these error paths where portable.; Rewrite the package-root unit around tracked `package.json` paths, compare the discovered set exactly with `PACKAGE_ROOTS ∪ PACKAGE_ROOTS_EXCLUDE`, prove disjointness, and assert the exact AM-40 arrays are sorted and unique without silently deleting a possible root package.; Add a code-weaver fixture for a used named import absent from the target's seeded exports and assert the appropriate code-symbol-to-repository-file or repository-file-to-repository-file fallback locator and edge, as applicable.

codex has hard stops: The shared lexical classifier does not implement the exact D33 accepted-form grammar. Its regex/character heuristic can omit real accepted edges—for example, after postfix `++`, `/` is treated as a regex introducer, so valid code such as `x++ / import("./helper.mjs")` masks the literal dynamic import. Its unbounded keyword-to-`from` search can also associate `import`/`export` property names with later, unrelated tokens. Closure equality against this classifier therefore does not prove equality with the normative D33 closure.; The Phase-1 scanner is not declaration-aware: `scanExports("const o = { export: 1 };")` emits an unsupported-export warning even though there is no export declaration. Likewise, `scanImports` records a span only through the specifier literal rather than through the complete import declaration, so identifiers in import attributes/assertions can be mistaken for uses of an imported local binding, violating the requirement that use occur outside the import declaration and that unused imports emit no edge.; The resolver/containment substrate is not fully fail-closed or canonical. `resolveRelativeSpecifier` and `deriveAcceptedClosure` return paths produced by `toPosix(path.relative(...))` without applying `isCanonicalRepoRelPosix`, so noncanonical repository-relative names can enter a closure. Separately, several `lstatSync` catch-all paths treat every filesystem error as absence: `walkFiles` can silently omit an entry, and `physicalContainment` can stop at an inaccessible component and return true based on an earlier ancestor. An unclassifiable filesystem error must not establish containment or a complete walk.; The PACKAGE_ROOTS proof does not implement AM-40's mandated tracked-file invariant. It scans the working filesystem rather than tracked `package.json` files, unconditionally removes the repository-root directory, skips selected directory names, and does not pin the exact five included and three excluded arrays or their sorted/unique form. Thus it is not the required mechanical proof that the tracked package directories equal the committed union with an empty intersection.; The required code-weaver proof is incomplete: no fixture exercises a used named import whose target is a real seeded module but whose imported name is not a seeded export, proving the mandated file-level fallback for that specific case.

acceptance.note: Fail-closed: see gate.blockers and seat decisions.
